qids.subject: Programming in C
qids.prose: Consider the following C functions. int fun1(int n) { int fun2(int n) { static int i= 0; static int i= 0; if (n > 0) { if (n>0) { ++i; i = i+ fun1 (n) ; fun1(n-1); fun2(n-1) ; } } return (i); return (i); } } The return value of is _________

qids.subject: Programming in C
qids.prose: #include <stdio.h> int foo(int S[],int size) { if(size == 0) return 0; if(size == 1) return 1; if(S[0] != S[1]) return 1+foo(S+1,size-1); return foo(S+1,size-1); } int main() { int A[]={0,1,2,2,2,0,0,1,1}; printf("%d",foo(A,9)); return 0; }

qids.subject: Programming in C
qids.prose: The function f is defined as follows: int f (int n) { if (n <= 1) return 1; else if (n % 2 == 0) return f(n/2); else return f(3n - 1); } Assuming that arbitrarily large integers can be passed as a parameter to the function, consider the following statements. i.
